title: Luscious Kenyan Bread Casserole
description: A roasted, gooey, kid-friendly Kenyan dish featuring rum and long-fermentation dough baked in a Dutch oven.
instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, yeast, sugar, salt, and warm water; stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 8 minutes until smooth, then add olive oil and continue kneading for 2 minutes.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise at room temperature for 3 hours, or until doubled in size.
Preheat your oven to 375°F and place a Dutch oven with lid on the middle rack.
After the dough has risen, punch it down, shape it into a round loaf, and place it in the Dutch oven.
In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat, then add cream, evaporated milk, rum, vanilla, and beaten eggs; whisk until smooth and pour over the loaf.
Cover with the Dutch oven lid, bake for 30 minutes, then remove the lid and bake for an additional 15 minutes until golden brown and bubbly.
Let the casserole cool for 10 minutes before slicing; serve warm.
